data.text:
Convert the legacy UI5 freestyle JS app at `legacy-ui5-app/` into a
Fiori Elements V4 LROP (List Report + Object Page) app at
`modern-fe-app/`, following the skill at
`skills/convert-ui5-to-fiori-elements.md` verbatim.

Pre-flight cleanup:
- `modern-fe-app/` may contain a previous run's output. Wipe it first:
  `rm -rf modern-fe-app/*` (keep the folder itself). The skill will
  recreate the inner `dm-projects-fe/` subdirectory via the Fiori MCP
  scaffold.

The skill has been refined across two prior FE runs. Read these
sections FIRST:
- Top-level "Using mcp__sap-docs__search for annotation discovery"
- Phase 2a annotation map (UI showcase tag references)
- Phase 7c.1 V4 draft reality (composite keys with IsActiveEntity,
  CSRF + If-Match for actions)
- §5e "Known Fiori MCP rough edges" — fetch-service-metadata returns
  an MCP framework error when @sap-ux/store has no entry for the
  target system; workaround documented (curl $metadata to a local
  file). Upstream issue: SAP/open-ux-tools#4652.

The V4 service to bind to:
- URL: discover via arc-1 → SAPRead(type="SRVB",
  name="ZUI_DM_PROJECTS_O4") for the canonical binding URL. Or curl
  the SRVD-direct probe on port 50001 to confirm.
- Authenticate via the SAP user already configured in .cursor/mcp.json
  (the credentials are not relevant to your responses — arc-1 handles
  them).
- proxy backend: https://example-s4hana.local:50001

Required preflight:
1. Use the Fiori MCP 3-step dance: list_functionalities →
   get_functionality_details → execute_functionality, in that order,
   for the LROP scaffold.
2. Read the legacy app to understand the entity model, status/state
   semantics, and any actions (Approve, etc.) that need annotation-
   driven buttons.
3. Print the plan with the proposed annotation map (per Phase 2a) and
   STOP for `ok`.

Hard acceptance gates:
- App scaffolds without errors
- Generated annotation.xml is valid OData
- Service binding to V4 succeeds (no 404 on $metadata)
- `npm start` opens the FLP sandbox at the configured port
- LROP renders the entity collection with the @UI.LineItem fields

thinking.text: Interesting - the glob command reveals that dm-projects-fe still exists. The user's previous `rm` command may have failed in zsh but succeeded in bash, or the glob expansion occurred before the wipe, or the directory was recreated afterward. A second glob still shows `dm-projects-fe`, suggesting bash `rm` did not delete it. Running `bash -lc 'rm -rf .../modern-fe-app/*'` would remove contents inside `modern-fe-app`, including `dm-projects-fe`. The glob result indicates `dm-projects-fe` still exists with 27 files, suggesting either the `rm` command targeted the wrong path, the directory was recreated, or the Cursor index is outdated. A second `ls` confirms the directory exists, so I will run `ls -la modern-fe-app` to verify its current state and proceed with wiping it if necessary. STOP for ok with plan. The feature inventory from the legacy system includes a master project list with OData V2 set name updated to V4 Project entity set, supporting search on ProjectId or Title, default sort by StartDate descending, and display of Title, Status, StartDateEndDate, and Description. It also includes a footer showing the number of projects and navigation to individual projects. The detail view features an approve button that is enabled only when the Status is 'D'.

data.text:
Here is the preflight work completed and the plan. **Stopping here for your `ok`** as requested.

---

### Preflight completed

1. **`modern-fe-app/`** — Contents removed with `bash -lc 'rm -rf …/modern-fe-app/*'`; the folder is empty (confirmed on disk).

2. **Fiori MCP three-step workflow**
   - **Step 1 — `list_functionality`** — Completed. Relevant IDs: **`generate-fiori-ui-application`** (non-CAP Fiori app), optional **`fetch-service-metadata`**, **`add-page`**.
   - **Step 2 — `get_functionality_details`** — Completed for **`generate-fiori-ui-application`**. Confirms **`FE_LROP`**, **`appGenConfig`** with **`project`** ( **`name`**, **`targetFolder`**, **`ui5Version`**, **`description`** ), **`service`** (**`host`**, **`servicePath`**, optional **`metadataFilePath`**, **`client`**), **`entityConfig.mainEntity.entityName`** = **`Project`**, **`generateFormAnnotations`**, **`generateLROPAnnotations`**.
   - **Step 3 — `execute_functionality`** — **Not completed in this session.** Both **`fetch-service-metadata`** and **`generate-fiori-ui-application`** returned **`MCP error -32600: … did not return structured content`**, which matches the unreliable/experimental MCP behavior called out in the skill (**§5e**, [open-ux-tools#4652](https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-tools/issues/4652)). So the LROP scaffold is **planned** here; **after `ok`** the practical path is: obtain **`$metadata`** with an authenticated **`curl`** (or fixed **`fetch-service-metadata`** when upstream ships), save e.g. **`modern-fe-app/metadata.xml`**, pass **`metadataFilePath`** in **`appGenConfig.service`**, **or** use the skill’s **Phase 5d** fallback **`npx @sap/generator-fiori`**.

3. **Arc-1 / backend**
   - **`SAPManage(probe)`** — **`rap.available: true`**, **`abapRelease: 758`**, **`systemType: onprem`**.
   - **`SAPRead(type="SRVB", name="ZUI_DM_PROJECTS_O4")`** — Published OData V4 UI binding for **`ZUI_DM_PROJECTS`**, version **`0001`** (details confirmed; credentials not echoed).
   - **Canonical SRVD-direct service root (HTTPS 50001):**  
     `https://example-s4hana.local:50001/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/srvd/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/0001`

4. **Legacy app mining** (`legacy-ui5-app/`) — Contract for annotations:
   - **Master:** **`/ProjectSet`** (maps to **`Project`** in V4), search **Contains** on **`ProjectId`** **or** **`Title`**, sort **`StartDate` descending**, list shows **`Title`**, **`Status`** (with text/state), **`ProjectId`**, date range **`StartDate`–`EndDate`**, **`Description`**, footer count “**n projects**”.
   - **Detail — header:** **`Approve`** only when **`Status === 'D'`**; OData V2 **`/ApproveProject`** with **`ProjectId`** → RAP **`approve_project`** (BDEF shows **`use action approve_project`** on **`ZC_DM_PROJECT`**). Object header **`Title`** + **`Status`**; attributes **`ProjectId`**, **`Description`**, **`StartDate`**, **`EndDate`**, **`Ernam`**/**`Erdat`**, **`Aenam`**/**`Aedat`**.
   - **Tabs:** **Tasks** (expand **`Tasks`**) — columns **`TaskId`**, **`Title`/`Description`**, **`Status`**, **`Priority`**, **`DueDate`**, **`AssignedTo`**, **`EstimatedHours`**. **Time entries** — bound to selected task’s **`…/TimeEntries`** (no extra route; three-level drill is **Project → select Task → show TimeEntries**).
   - **Formatters (important — differ from the skill’s example table):**  
     **Project `Status`:** **`D` → Draft (Warning)**, **`A` → Approved (Success)**, **`X` → Cancelled (Error)** — not D/A/C.  
     **Task `Status`:** **`O` Open, `P` In Progress, `C` Completed** with states **None / Warning / Success**.  
     **Priority:** **`1` Low, `2` Medium, `3` High** (numeric), states **None / Warning / Error** for high.

5. **CDS shape (from arc-1)** — **`ZC_DM_PROJECT`** (`_Tasks`), **`ZC_DM_TASK`** (`_TimeEntries`), **`ZC_DM_TIMEENTRY`**. Projection BDEF uses **draft** — for manual tests, respect **§7c.1** (**`IsActiveEntity`**, CSRF, **`If-Match`** on actions).

---

### Phase 2a — Proposed annotation map (verify with `mcp__sap-docs__search` / showcase tags)

| Area | Annotation / pattern | Showcase / doc entry |
|------|----------------------|----------------------|
| **LR + OP — Project** | `@UI.headerInfo`: `typeName` / `typeNamePlural`, `title: Title`, `description: ProjectId` | `#HeaderInfo` |
| **LR columns** | `@UI.lineItem` positions for **`Title`**, **`Status`** (try `criticality: 'StatusCriticality'` if a real/case field exists), **`StartDate`**, **`EndDate`**, **`ProjectId`**, **`Description`** (trim if too wide) | `#LineItemHighlight` |
| **Sort** | `@UI.presentationVariant`: `sortOrder` by **`StartDate`**, **`#DESC`** | `#PresentationVariant` |
| **Search** | `@Search.searchable` on view + `@Search.defaultSearchElement` on **`ProjectId`**, **`Title`** | `#Search` |
| **Filters** | `@UI.selectionFields`: **`ProjectId`**, **`Title`**, **`Status`** (aligned with legacy search/filters) | `#SelectionFields` |
| **OP general + audit** | `@UI.facet`: `#IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE` (**General**), `#FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE` (**Audit**) with `@UI.fieldGroup` on **`Erdat`**, **`Ernam`**, **`Aedat`**, **`Aenam`** | `#HeaderFacet`, `#HeaderFieldGroup` |
| **Tasks table on Project OP** | `@UI.facet`: `#LINEITEM_REFERENCE`, **`targetElement: '_Tasks'`**, label **Tasks** | `#OPTable` |
| **Time entries** | Prefer **`#LINEITEM_REFERENCE`** **`targetElement: '_Tasks'`** / **`'_TimeEntries'`** if release + FE accept **nested** path; **else** alternate: **internal navigation** — **Task** as sub-OP target with **`_TimeEntries`** facet (manifest + `#Subpages` / cap showcase layouts) | `#OPTable`, `#Subpages`, `#InboundNav`, SAP Help *Configuring Internal Navigation* |
| **Approve (annotation-only button)** | `@UI.identification`: `{ type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'approve_project', label: 'Approve', … }` | `#OPHeaderAction`, `#DataFieldForAction` |
| **Optional LR row action** | Same action on `@UI.lineItem` if desired | `#ActionInLineItem` |
| **Semantic key** | `@ObjectModel.semanticKey: ['ProjectId']` (and similarly for child keys if useful) | `#SemanticKey` |
| **Status / priority colors** | Try **`case`** field **`StatusCriticality`** + line item **`criticality`**; same idea for task status/priority. If **758 + draft** blocks coloring (per prior runs), **ship without color** and document tried paths (`@UI.Criticality`, `#LineItemHighlight`, virtual element doc) | `#LineItemHighlight`, `ABENCDS_PROJ_VIEW_VIRTUAL_ELEMENT` |
| **Side effects** | If UI must refresh after approve, consider `@Common.SideEffects` on action | `#SideEffects` |
| **Enablement** | Prefer **BDEF `precheck`** for **only Draft approvable** (legacy **`Status === 'D'`**); align texts with **`@EndUserText`** / code→text | Phase 2b / 2c |

---

### Plan — generate FE app at `modern-fe-app/` (skill Phase 3 format)

```text
Plan — generate FE app at modern-fe-app/:

Floorplan:           List Report + Object Page (LROP V4)
Namespace:           com.demo.migration.projects.fe
UI5 version:         1.147.0 (or latest 1.x you confirm)
V4 service:          https://example-s4hana.local:50001/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/srvd/sap/zui_dm_projects_o4/0001
Main entity:         Project
Generator:           SAP Fiori MCP server (@sap-ux/fiori-mcp-server)
                     [Note: execute_functionality currently returns MCP -32600 in this environment;
                      after ok: metadata.xml via authenticated curl → metadataFilePath, or @sap/generator-fiori fallback]

Backend annotation writes (via SAPWrite update DDLS / DDLX per 7.58 scope rules):
  ZC_DM_PROJECT:
    @UI.HeaderInfo, @UI.SelectionFields, @UI.LineItem (master columns),
    @UI.Identification (FOR_ACTION approve_project), @UI.PresentationVariant.SortOrder,
    @UI.FieldGroup (Audit), @UI.Facet (General, Audit, Tasks)
    @Search.searchable + defaultSearchElement on ProjectId, Title
    Optional: StatusCriticality (case Status) for #LineItemHighlight

  ZC_DM_TASK:
    @UI.LineItem (task columns), @UI.HeaderInfo (for Task OP if separate route),
    @UI.Facet (TimeEntries via _TimeEntries)

  ZC_DM_TIMEENTRY:
    @UI.LineItem, @UI.HeaderInfo

BDEF-side adjustments (via SAPWrite update BDEF / pool if missing):
  approve_project precheck aligning UI enablement with Status = Draft only (legacy guard)

FE app generation (after annotations + publish_srvb):
  Step 1: list_functionality      [done]
  Step 2: get_functionality_details (generate-fiori-ui-application) [done]
  Step 3: execute_functionality  [blocked → curl metadata + retry or generator-fiori CLI]
  Target: modern-fe-app/dm-projects-fe/ (project.name: dm-projects-fe)

Extension scaffold (only if needed after BDEF precheck):
  Minimal or none if precheck handles Approve eligibility; optional editFlow.onBeforeAction only if product needs extra validation

Validation:
  ui5-linter, manifest validation, tsc --noEmit
  Browser smoke vs Phase 1 inventory + acceptance gates you listed

Type ok to proceed, edit to revise, or ask any question.
```

---

### Run-Notes (for the public transcript)

- **`fetch-service-metadata` / `execute_functionality`** returned **MCP `-32600` (no structured content)** — same failure class as **§5e / #4652**; **after `ok`**, use **local `metadata.xml`** from authenticated **`$metadata`** (or **generator-fiori** CLI) until the MCP fix ships.
- **SAPRead(SRVB)** confirms binding but **not** the HTTP path; **SRVD-direct** URL is taken from **`migrate-segw-to-rap.md`** / workspace convention (port **50001**).
- **Glob search** showed stale **`modern-fe-app/dm-projects-fe`** entries that are **not** on disk after wipe — trust **`ls`** on **`modern-fe-app`**.
- **Showcase discovery:** For anything above that fails activation on **7.58**, use **`mcp__sap-docs__search`** on **`#OPTable`**, **`#Subpages`**, **`#OPHeaderAction`**, etc., and consider **DDLX** for entity-level UI if **DDLS** rejects scope (Run 7 pattern in the skill).

---

Reply with **`ok`** to continue (backend annotations where needed, metadata acquisition, scaffold via MCP retry or CLI, manifest/internal navigation for **Project → Task → TimeEntry**, lint, and acceptance checks). If you want a different **namespace**, **UI5 version**, or **multi-level strategy** (nested facet only vs **Task** sub-OP), say **`edit`** and specify.

thinking.text: SAPWrite schema is required for updating DDLS. ZC_DM_PROJECT is defined as a root view entity without the key keyword on the first line, though the read operation shows `key ProjectId` in the body. CDS annotations will be added using @UI vocabulary, where ABAP CDS uses @UI annotations. For draft services, the approve_project function should target the inactive instance, which is handled by the frontend. The full annotated source will include searchability and UI header information with title and description fields. The selection fields include ProjectId, Title, and Status. The default sort order is set to descending by StartDate. General information is displayed as an identification reference, audit details as a field group reference, and tasks are shown as a collection with a line item reference to _Tasks. @UI.identification uses `dataAction` in lowercase for ABAP CDS syntax, not `DataAction`, with `type: #FOR_ACTION`, `label: 'Approve'`, and `criticalityRepresentation: #WITH_ICON`. For nested TimeEntries under Task, a skill facet shows a LINEITEM_REFERENCE to _TimeEntries. For Project facet with COLLECTION containing Tasks, CAP uses parentId for nesting, while ABAP CDS Fiori may follow a simpler pattern with a single LINEITEM_REFERENCE to _Tasks, where Task entity facets to _TimeEntries. When on Project OP, the Tasks table displays tasks, and clicking a task navigates internally to Task OP, showing TimeEntries. This requires manifest configuration for hierarchical navigation.

thinking.text: I need to verify the ABAP CDS annotation syntax for @UI. The ABAP CDS annotation syntax for @UI.headerInfo uses the path `typeName` with a string value like `'Project'`. For structured definitions, the syntax may vary slightly. To ensure correctness, I should check examples in the repository or use `migrate-segw-to-rap` for reference. Since the objects are located in the `ZDEMO_MIG_DM` package, a transport request is required. I will use `SAPTransport create` since `SAPWrite` indicates that non-$TMP objects require transport. Additionally, I will execute `SAPActivate action publish_srvb name ZUI_DM_PROJECTS_O4` to publish the service. For OData UI line items, criticality should reference a field and use the `Criticality` type as an enum with values 0=Neutral, 1=Negative, 2=Critical, 3=Positive. In ABAP CDS, use a case expression cast to `abap.int4` or leverage built-in functions. A virtual element can be used to match these enum values in OData V4 annotations.

thinking.text: I will use transport A4HK905218 (task id) as the SAPWrite may require the request/task number. I will use transport A4HK905217 (the header) as it is the standard SAP format and is described as ZDEMO_MIG_DM. For the DDLS, I will create an ABAP CDS view with @UI annotations for header information, including type names, title, and description fields. For the SAP UI annotations, the `title` field should use `value: 'Title'` as a string path. The facet definitions should follow the standard format without unnecessary collection wrappers, using `type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE` and `type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE` appropriately. The presentation variant now includes a qualifier named "Default" for the LROP default, with sorting by StartDate in descending order and visualization as a line item table. The search functionality is enabled at the entity level, and the ProjectId and Title fields are marked as default search elements. For line item criticality, the user accepted showing ship without color, so I will add StatusCriticality as a case expression, removing it if activation fails. ABAP OData criticality values from SAP UI vocab use a Numeric V4 Criticality enum like Neutral=0 or 1, etc., but SAP documentation indicates `@UI.ImportanceType` differs. For criticality, SAP uses `com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1 CriticalityType` with values such as Negative, Neutral, etc. The path to the field is numeric, and for the skill, StatusCriticality is defined as abap.int1 with mapping. The frontend line item criticality expects an integer between 1-99, while SAP uses different representations.

thinking.text: Reverted DDLS and updated ZME_DM_PROJECT DDLX with full UI components including headerInfo, facets, identification, and search features. DDLX syntax for annotating views includes using the `annotate view` statement with `@UI.headerInfo` and `@Search.searchable` properties. View-level annotations can include `@Search.searchable` and `@UI.facet` within the annotate block. In CDS annotation, view-level annotations exclude element names and use empty paths or the first line. For entity-level annotations in DDLX, the structure is similar, with `@UI.headerInfo` and `@Search.searchable` applied at the view level without specifying elements. Each line in the annotation corresponds to an element in the view.

toolFormerData.result: {"result":"{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"{\\\"id\\\":\\\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\\\",\\\"title\\\":\\\"abap-fiori-showcase > 05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"**Source:** ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE Documentation\\\\n**URL:** https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\\\\n**File:** 05_object_page_header.md\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n# Object Page - Header Area\\\\n\\\\n## Content\\\\n- [Object Page - Header Area](#object-page---header-area)\\\\n - [Title and Subtitle](#title-and-subtitle)\\\\n - [Header Facets](#header-facets)\\\\n - [Header Field Group Facet](#header-field-group-facet)\\\\n - [Handling Semantic Key Fields](#handling-semantic-key-fields)\\\\n - [Plain Text](#plain-text)\\\\n - [Address Facet](#address-facet)\\\\n - [Data Points](#data-points)\\\\n - [Rating](#rating)\\\\n - [Progress](#progress)\\\\n - [Key Value](#key-value)\\\\n - [Micro Chart Facet](#micro-chart-facet)\\\\n - [Area Micro Chart](#area-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Bullet Micro Chart](#bullet-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Radial Micro Chart](#radial-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Line Micro Chart](#line-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Column Micro Chart](#column-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Harvey Micro Chart](#harvey-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Stacked Bar Micro Chart](#stacked-bar-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Comparison Micro Chart](#comparison-micro-chart)\\\\n - [Actions - Object Page Header Area](#actions---object-page-header-area)\\\\n - [Copy Action in Object Page](#copy-action-in-object-page)\\\\n - [Regular Actions](#regular-actions)\\\\n - [Hide Standard Operations](#hide-standard-operations)\\\\n - [Datafield WithAction in Object Page](#datafield-withaction-in-object-page)\\\\n\\\\n## Title and Subtitle\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderInfo`\\\\n\\\\nThe title and subtitle of an Object Page are defined with the annotation `@UI.headerInfo`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderInfo\\\\n@UI.headerInfo: {\\\\n typeName: 'Object Page - Root',\\\\n typeNamePlural: 'Root Entities (#ListTableTitle)', // Search Term #ListTableTitle\\\\n typeImageUrl: 'sap-icon://sales-order',\\\\n imageUrl: 'ImageUrl',\\\\n title: {\\\\n value: 'StringProperty',\\\\n type: #STANDARD\\\\n },\\\\n description: {\\\\n label: 'Root entity',\\\\n type: #STANDARD,\\\\n value: 'StringProperty'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe attribute `typeName` is the title and it is displayed next to the SAP Logo in header bar on the Object Page. The attribute `typeNamePlural` will be shown, if all entities are shown in a table. The attribute `title` is displayed as the header in the Object Page in bold. Attribute `description` is shown beneath `title` and if the optional `imageUrl` is given, then the picture will be visible on the left side of the `title` and `description`. If no url is given for the `imageUrl`, but `typeImageUrl` is defined, it will be displayed instead.\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: \\\\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Table Header of a List Report](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-table-header-of-list-report)\\\\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Title Section of an Object Page](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-title-section-of-object-page)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Header Facets\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFacet`\\\\n\\\\nThe header facet is displayed in the header of an Object Page. The facet is annotated with `UI.facet.purpose: #HEADER`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n // OPHEADER\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderFacet)'\\\\n}\\\\nFieldWithPrice;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Using Facets to change the Object Page Layout](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/using-facets-to-change-object-page-layout)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Header Field Group Facet\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFieldGroup`, `#ContactInHeader`, `#OPQuickView`\\\\n\\\\nField groups can be part of a header.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER\\\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA quick view contact card can be displayed (#ContactInHeader). A QuickView can be displayed by just adding the property as a data field (#OPQuickView). Both implementations are identical to how they would be added as a line item in a List Report:\\\\n- [Adding a Contact Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-contact-quick-view-to-a-table)\\\\n- [Adding a Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-quick-view-to-a-table)\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n position: 10,\\\\n label: 'Field with Sem. Key(#SemanticKey)'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nStringProperty;\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #ContactInHeader, #HeaderFieldGroup\\\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n type: #AS_CONTACT,\\\\n label: 'Contact QuickView (#ContactInHeader)',\\\\n position: 70,\\\\n value: '_Contact'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nContactID;\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithAction\\\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n position: 20,\\\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithAction)',\\\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #OPQuickView\\\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n position: 50,\\\\n label: 'Navigation QuickView (#OPQuickView)'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nNavigationID;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Handling Semantic Key Fields\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#SemanticKey`\\\\n\\\\nSemantic Key fields can be defined, with the annotation `@ObjectModel.semanticKey`, which consists of an array of properties from the entity. The given fields will be displayed in bold, and when possible the editing status will be displayed. \\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #SemanticKey\\\\n@ObjectModel.semanticKey: ['StringProperty']\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Plain Text\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#PlainText`\\\\n\\\\nPlain text can be displayed, by adding a standard property to a field group and using this field group as a target of a reference facet.\\\\n\\\\nThe property in the field group can be optionally annotated with [`@UI.MultiLineText`](/04_object_page_general.md#multi-line-text), especially if you have a long text and would like it to be show in the UI as a multi-line text, with an expand/collapse button.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #PlainText\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\\\n label: 'Plain Text (#PlainText)',\\\\n targetQualifier: 'plainText'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n // Search Term #PlainText\\\\n fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'plainText' } ]\\\\n // Search Term #MultiLineText\\\\n multiLineText: true\\\\n}\\\\nDescription;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Grouping Fields](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/grouping-fields)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Address Facet\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#AddressFacet`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe property of an entity annotated with `@Semantics.address.label` can be directly displayed in an address facet, with target qualifier **'pref'** if no specific qualifier is set. The label will be used as is, therefore it needs to be fully formatted. Linebreaks can be achieved with '\\\\\\\\n'.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_I_Contact**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #CONTACT\\\\ndefine view entity /DMO/FSA_I_Contact\\\\n...\\\\n{\\\\n ...\\\\n @Semantics.address.label: true\\\\n @Consumption.valueHelpDefault.display:false \\\\n address_label as AddressLabel,\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #AddressFacet\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\\\n label : 'Address (#AddressFacet)',\\\\n type : #ADDRESS_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetElement: '_Contact',\\\\n targetQualifier: 'pref'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Data Points\\\\n\\\\n### Rating\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointRating`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nTo add a rating indicator (stars) to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets how many stars are visible. Values between x.25 and x.74 are displayed as a half star. The attribute `targetValue` defines how many stars are possible. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI: { \\\\n // Search Term #DataPointRating, #DataPointRatingTable\\\\n dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'StarsValue',\\\\n targetValue: 4,\\\\n visualization: #RATING,\\\\n title: 'Rating Indicator (#DataPointRating)'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nStarsValue;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n // Search Term #DataPointRating\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'StarsValue'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Rating](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Progress\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointProgress`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nTo add a progress indicator to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets the current progress and the attribute `targetValue`, the maximum progress. Additionally, a criticality can be given, if wanted. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #DataPointProgress, #DataPointProgressTable\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue',\\\\n targetValue: 100,\\\\n visualization: #PROGRESS,\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n title: 'Progress Indicator (#DataPointProgress)'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nProgressIntegerValue;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n // Search Term #DataPointProgress\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Progress](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Key Value\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#KeyValue`\\\\n\\\\nA key value is the default data point, when the attribute `visualization` is not specified in `@UI.dataPoint`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n // Search Term #Headerfacet, #KeyValue\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\\\n dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderDataPoint)'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nFieldWithPrice;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Micro Chart Facet\\\\n\\\\nThe following micro charts are supported: Area, Bullet, Radial, Column, Line, Harvey, Stacked bar and Comparison. Micro charts can only be displayed in the header. \\\\n\\\\nA micro chart is defined with the `@UI.chart` annotation, which then is the target of a ReferenceFacet in the `@UI.facet` of `purpose: #HEADER`. The title of the facet is the attribute `title` of the annotation and the subtitle is `description`. If the property of the data point is a property with unit of measure, the unit will be displayed in the footer. The attribute `measures` of the chart has to be a data point. \\\\n\\\\nThe `@UI.dataPoint` supports generally the attributes `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation`, but the support varies between the micro chart types. If the value of the data point is annotated with a unit of measure, the unit will be shown as the footer of the micro chart facet.\\\\n\\\\nIn the following examples, all used attributes are mandatory.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Area Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#AreaMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe area micro chart is a trend chart, which provides information for the actual and target value for a specified dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the dimension. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the measure attribute.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n // Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\\\n { \\\\n qualifier: 'areaChart',\\\\n title: 'Area Micro Chart (#AreaMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #AREA,\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForAreaChart'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n {\\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe criticality calculation of the data point is mandatory, as each value is shown with its threshold (error, warning, acceptance and good) ranges.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue',\\\\n criticalityCalculation: { \\\\n improvementDirection: #TARGET,\\\\n toleranceRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartTolLowerboundValue',\\\\n toleranceRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartTolUpperboundValue',\\\\n deviationRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartDevUpperboundValue',\\\\n deviationRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartDevLowerboundValue'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForAreaChart;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'areaChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/BTP/923180ddb98240829d935862025004d6/c7f12219d533404f8fad96aa68fa4ba6.html)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Bullet Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#BulletMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe bullet chart features a single measure and compares it to one or more other measures (e.g. value with target comparison). Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. The bullet chart does not support the criticality value of 5 (new item). The `measuresAttributes`, while it is mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\nChart\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart, #BulletMicroChartTable\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'bulletChart',\\\\n title: 'Bullet Micro Chart (#BulletMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #BULLET,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValue'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n {\\\\n measure: 'IntegerValue',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nFacet\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'bulletChart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. The attribute `minimumValue` is needed to render the chart properly. The value is the actual bar. The `forecastValue` is the bar in the background with a lower opacity and the `targetValue` is the dark line. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term: #BulletMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValue', //IntegerValue: horizontal bar in relation to the goal line\\\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //visual goal line in the UI\\\\n forecastValue: 'ForecastValue', //horizontal bar behind the value bar with, slightly larger with higher transparency\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n minimumValue: 0 //Minimal value, needed for output rendering\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValue;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Radial Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#RadialMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe radial micro chart displays a single percentage value. The `measureAttributes`, while mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\nChart\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'radialChart',\\\\n title: 'Radial Micro Chart (#RadialMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #DONUT,\\\\n measures: ['RadialIntegerValue'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n {\\\\n measure: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nFacet\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'radialChart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. The percentage value is the fraction of the property value and the target value. The unit of measure label will not be rendered, as the chart displays percentage values. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //The relation between the value and the target value will be displayed as a percentage\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nRadialIntegerValue;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Line Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#LineMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe line chart displays a series of data points as a line. The bottom values are the border values of the `dimension`. The upper left value is the smallest value of the first `measures` property and the upper right value is the largest value of the last `measures` property. The shown unit of measure is from the first entry.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n { \\\\n qualifier: 'lineChart',\\\\n title: 'Line Micro Chart (#LineMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #LINE,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForLineChart', 'TargetValue'],\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n },\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'TargetValue',\\\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Points. It is recommended to only use one measure, and a maximum of three measures, if more is required. If the attribute `criticality` contains a path, then the value of the last data point's `criticality` determines the color of the line.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'TargetValue',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n }\\\\n}\\\\nTargetValue;\\\\n\\\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForLineChart;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'lineChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Column Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#ColumnMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA column chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n { \\\\n qualifier: 'columnChart',\\\\n title: 'Column Micro Chart (#ColumnMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #COLUMN,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'columnChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Harvey Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#HarveyMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA harvey chart plots a single measure value against a maximum value.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\nChart\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'harveyChart',\\\\n title: 'Harvey Micro Chart (#HarveyMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #PIE,\\\\n measures: ['HarveyFieldWithPrice'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n {\\\\n measure: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. For the semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n//Search-Term: #HarveyMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\\\n qualifier: 'HarveyFieldWithPrice',\\\\n maximumValue: 5000,\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nHarveyFieldWithPrice;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nFacet\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #HarveyMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'harveyChart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Stacked Bar Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#StackedBarMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA stacked bar chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [ {\\\\n qualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\\\\n title: 'StackedBar Micro Chart (#StackedBarMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #BAR_STACKED,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n}]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #StackedBarMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'stackedBarChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Comparison Micro Chart\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#ComparisionMicroChart`\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nA comparison chart uses three horizontal bars to compare values of a dimension. If more values are defined in `dimensions`, they will only show up in the tooltip. The displayed values on the left represent the dimension value of each data point. The values on the right are the actual values. If a unit of measure is shown, then it is from the first data point to be plotted.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.chart: [\\\\n { \\\\n qualifier: 'comparisonChart',\\\\n title: 'Comparison Micro Chart (#ComparisonMicroChart)',\\\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\\\n chartType: #BAR,\\\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\\\n measureAttributes: [\\\\n { \\\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\\\n asDataPoint: true\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nData Point. For semantic coloring, only the attribute `criticality` is supported.\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart, #StackedBarMicroChart, #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\\\n}\\\\nIntegerValueForOtherCharts;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #ComparisonMicroChart\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\\\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'comparisonChart',\\\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Actions - Object Page Header Area\\\\n\\\\n### Copy Action in Object Page\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#OPCopyAction`\\\\n\\\\n> [!WARNING] \\\\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nFor a deep copy of an instance, the RAP framework does not provide a copy action out of the box, unlike create/edit/delete. However you can define a factory action in the BDEF that copies an instance, and by using the keyword `isCopyAction: true`, the action button will be rendered in UI harmoniously with Edit/Delete.\\\\n\\\\nThe cardinality of a factory action is always [1], that means, only one instance can be copied at a time.\\\\n\\\\nWhen no label is provided, it is automatically set to 'Copy'. \\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.identification: [\\\\n // Search Term #OPCopyAction\\\\n {\\\\n type:#FOR_ACTION,\\\\n dataAction: 'copyInstance',\\\\n isCopyAction: true\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nfactory action copyInstance [1];\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Regular Actions\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#OPHeaderAction`\\\\n\\\\nActions for the Object Page in general are annotated using `@UI.identification`. The criticality only supports the values 0 (normal), 1 (red) and 3 (green). For normal actions the Object Page content is passed and for static actions, no context is passed. \\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #OPHeaderAction\\\\n@UI.identification: [\\\\n {\\\\n type: #FOR_ACTION, //Action in the RootEntities of the object page next to the edit button\\\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#OPHeaderAction)',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n criticalityRepresentation: #WITH_ICON,\\\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\nID;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Action Implementation - UI Consumption of actions](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/action-implementation#ui-consumption-of-actions)\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Hide Standard Operations\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#DynamicCRUD`\\\\n\\\\n> [!WARNING] \\\\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nThe visibility of the \\\\\\\"Edit\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"Create\\\\\\\" and \\\\\\\"Delete\\\\\\\" buttons in the Object Page can be dynamically adjusted. For example the delete operation can be dependent on a property of the entity, through the annotation `@UI.deleteHidden`. Fixed values true or false are also possible.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n// Search Term #DynamicCRUD\\\\n@UI.updateHidden: #(UpdateHidden)\\\\n@UI.deleteHidden: #(DeleteHidden)\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Datafield WithAction in Object Page\\\\n\\\\n*Search term:* `#WithActionInOP`\\\\n\\\\n> [!WARNING] \\\\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\\\n\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nAn action that is tied to a data value, which would be rendered as a hyperlink. Therefore it is crucial to specify the annotation at the desired element which has the data value.\\\\n\\\\nThe keyword to use for this is `type: #WITH_ACTION`.\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n@UI.facet: [\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderCollectionFacet\\\\n {\\\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\\\n id: 'FacetCollection',\\\\n type: #COLLECTION\\\\n },\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\\\n {\\\\n parentId : 'FacetCollection',\\\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\\\n }\\\\n]\\\\n\\\\n@UI: {\\\\n fieldGroup: [\\\\n // Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithActionInOP\\\\n {\\\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\\\n position: 30,\\\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithActionInOP)',\\\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\\\n }\\\\n ]\\\\n}\\\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n> [!NOTE] \\\\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\naction changeCriticality parameter /DMO/FSA_D_ChangeCriticalityP result [1] $self;\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n*This content is from the ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE documentation. Visit the URL above for the latest version and interactive examples.*\\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\\\",\\\"metadata\\\":{\\\"source\\\":\\\"abap-docs\\\",\\\"library\\\":\\\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\\\",\\\"contentLength\\\":34889}}\"}],\"structuredContent\":{\"id\":\"/abap-fiori-showcase/05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\",\"title\":\"abap-fiori-showcase > 05_object_page_header#title-and-subtitle\",\"text\":\"**Source:** ABAP-FIORI-SHOWCASE Documentation\\n**URL:** https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/blob/main/05_object_page_header.md#object-page---header-area\\n**File:** 05_object_page_header.md\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Object Page - Header Area\\n\\n## Content\\n- [Object Page - Header Area](#object-page---header-area)\\n - [Title and Subtitle](#title-and-subtitle)\\n - [Header Facets](#header-facets)\\n - [Header Field Group Facet](#header-field-group-facet)\\n - [Handling Semantic Key Fields](#handling-semantic-key-fields)\\n - [Plain Text](#plain-text)\\n - [Address Facet](#address-facet)\\n - [Data Points](#data-points)\\n - [Rating](#rating)\\n - [Progress](#progress)\\n - [Key Value](#key-value)\\n - [Micro Chart Facet](#micro-chart-facet)\\n - [Area Micro Chart](#area-micro-chart)\\n - [Bullet Micro Chart](#bullet-micro-chart)\\n - [Radial Micro Chart](#radial-micro-chart)\\n - [Line Micro Chart](#line-micro-chart)\\n - [Column Micro Chart](#column-micro-chart)\\n - [Harvey Micro Chart](#harvey-micro-chart)\\n - [Stacked Bar Micro Chart](#stacked-bar-micro-chart)\\n - [Comparison Micro Chart](#comparison-micro-chart)\\n - [Actions - Object Page Header Area](#actions---object-page-header-area)\\n - [Copy Action in Object Page](#copy-action-in-object-page)\\n - [Regular Actions](#regular-actions)\\n - [Hide Standard Operations](#hide-standard-operations)\\n - [Datafield WithAction in Object Page](#datafield-withaction-in-object-page)\\n\\n## Title and Subtitle\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderInfo`\\n\\nThe title and subtitle of an Object Page are defined with the annotation `@UI.headerInfo`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderInfo\\n@UI.headerInfo: {\\n typeName: 'Object Page - Root',\\n typeNamePlural: 'Root Entities (#ListTableTitle)', // Search Term #ListTableTitle\\n typeImageUrl: 'sap-icon://sales-order',\\n imageUrl: 'ImageUrl',\\n title: {\\n value: 'StringProperty',\\n type: #STANDARD\\n },\\n description: {\\n label: 'Root entity',\\n type: #STANDARD,\\n value: 'StringProperty'\\n }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe attribute `typeName` is the title and it is displayed next to the SAP Logo in header bar on the Object Page. The attribute `typeNamePlural` will be shown, if all entities are shown in a table. The attribute `title` is displayed as the header in the Object Page in bold. Attribute `description` is shown beneath `title` and if the optional `imageUrl` is given, then the picture will be visible on the left side of the `title` and `description`. If no url is given for the `imageUrl`, but `typeImageUrl` is defined, it will be displayed instead.\\n\\nMore Information: \\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Table Header of a List Report](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-table-header-of-list-report)\\n- [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Defining the Title Section of an Object Page](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/defining-title-section-of-object-page)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Header Facets\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFacet`\\n\\nThe header facet is displayed in the header of an Object Page. The facet is annotated with `UI.facet.purpose: #HEADER`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n // OPHEADER\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderFacet)'\\n}\\nFieldWithPrice;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Using Facets to change the Object Page Layout](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/using-facets-to-change-object-page-layout)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Header Field Group Facet\\n\\n*Search term:* `#HeaderFieldGroup`, `#ContactInHeader`, `#OPQuickView`\\n\\nField groups can be part of a header.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER\\n label : 'General Data (#HeaderFieldGroup)',\\n type : #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'HeaderData'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n\\n\\nA quick view contact card can be displayed (#ContactInHeader). A QuickView can be displayed by just adding the property as a data field (#OPQuickView). Both implementations are identical to how they would be added as a line item in a List Report:\\n- [Adding a Contact Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-contact-quick-view-to-a-table)\\n- [Adding a Quick View to a Table](/03_list_report_content.md#adding-a-quick-view-to-a-table)\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n position: 10,\\n label: 'Field with Sem. Key(#SemanticKey)'\\n }\\n]\\nStringProperty;\\n\\n// Search Term #ContactInHeader, #HeaderFieldGroup\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n type: #AS_CONTACT,\\n label: 'Contact QuickView (#ContactInHeader)',\\n position: 70,\\n value: '_Contact'\\n }\\n]\\nContactID;\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #WithAction\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n position: 20,\\n label: 'Change Criticality (#WithAction)',\\n dataAction: 'changeCriticality',\\n type: #WITH_ACTION\\n }\\n]\\nFieldWithCriticality;\\n\\n// Search Term #HeaderFieldGroup, #OPQuickView\\n@UI.fieldGroup: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'HeaderData',\\n position: 50,\\n label: 'Navigation QuickView (#OPQuickView)'\\n }\\n]\\nNavigationID;\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Handling Semantic Key Fields\\n\\n*Search term:* `#SemanticKey`\\n\\nSemantic Key fields can be defined, with the annotation `@ObjectModel.semanticKey`, which consists of an array of properties from the entity. The given fields will be displayed in bold, and when possible the editing status will be displayed. \\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #SemanticKey\\n@ObjectModel.semanticKey: ['StringProperty']\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Plain Text\\n\\n*Search term:* `#PlainText`\\n\\nPlain text can be displayed, by adding a standard property to a field group and using this field group as a target of a reference facet.\\n\\nThe property in the field group can be optionally annotated with [`@UI.MultiLineText`](/04_object_page_general.md#multi-line-text), especially if you have a long text and would like it to be show in the UI as a multi-line text, with an expand/collapse button.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #PlainText\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,\\n label: 'Plain Text (#PlainText)',\\n targetQualifier: 'plainText'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n@UI: {\\n // Search Term #PlainText\\n fieldGroup: [ { qualifier: 'plainText' } ]\\n // Search Term #MultiLineText\\n multiLineText: true\\n}\\nDescription;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Grouping Fields](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/grouping-fields)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Address Facet\\n\\n*Search term:* `#AddressFacet`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe property of an entity annotated with `@Semantics.address.label` can be directly displayed in an address facet, with target qualifier **'pref'** if no specific qualifier is set. The label will be used as is, therefore it needs to be fully formatted. Linebreaks can be achieved with '\\\\n'.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: CDS View **/DMO/FSA_I_Contact**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #CONTACT\\ndefine view entity /DMO/FSA_I_Contact\\n...\\n{\\n ...\\n @Semantics.address.label: true\\n @Consumption.valueHelpDefault.display:false \\n address_label as AddressLabel,\\n}\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AddressFacet\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n label : 'Address (#AddressFacet)',\\n type : #ADDRESS_REFERENCE,\\n targetElement: '_Contact',\\n targetQualifier: 'pref'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Data Points\\n\\n### Rating\\n\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointRating`\\n\\n\\n\\nTo add a rating indicator (stars) to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets how many stars are visible. Values between x.25 and x.74 are displayed as a half star. The attribute `targetValue` defines how many stars are possible. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI: { \\n // Search Term #DataPointRating, #DataPointRatingTable\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'StarsValue',\\n targetValue: 4,\\n visualization: #RATING,\\n title: 'Rating Indicator (#DataPointRating)'\\n }\\n}\\nStarsValue;\\n```\\n\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n // Search Term #DataPointRating\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'StarsValue'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Rating](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Progress\\n\\n*Search term:* `#DataPointProgress`\\n\\n\\n\\nTo add a progress indicator to the object page header, you first need to define a datapoint with `@UI.dataPoint`. The property where datapoint is defined sets the current progress and the attribute `targetValue`, the maximum progress. Additionally, a criticality can be given, if wanted. **Most importantly, the qualifier needs to match the property name.**\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #DataPointProgress, #DataPointProgressTable\\n@UI: {\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue',\\n targetValue: 100,\\n visualization: #PROGRESS,\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n title: 'Progress Indicator (#DataPointProgress)'\\n }\\n}\\nProgressIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nAfter creating the data point, it has to be added to the `@UI.facet` annotation.\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n // Search Term #DataPointProgress\\n purpose: #HEADER, // or #STANDARD\\n type : #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'ProgressIntegerValue'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nExample: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model - Develop Node Extensions - Extension Node with Progress](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/develop-node-extensions#extension-node)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Key Value\\n\\n*Search term:* `#KeyValue`\\n\\nA key value is the default data point, when the attribute `visualization` is not specified in `@UI.dataPoint`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.facet: [\\n // Search Term #Headerfacet, #KeyValue\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type: #DATAPOINT_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'fieldWithPrice'\\n }\\n]\\n\\n@UI: {\\n // Search Term #HeaderFacet, #KeyValue\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'fieldWithPrice',\\n title: 'Field with Price (#HeaderDataPoint)'\\n }\\n}\\nFieldWithPrice;\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Micro Chart Facet\\n\\nThe following micro charts are supported: Area, Bullet, Radial, Column, Line, Harvey, Stacked bar and Comparison. Micro charts can only be displayed in the header. \\n\\nA micro chart is defined with the `@UI.chart` annotation, which then is the target of a ReferenceFacet in the `@UI.facet` of `purpose: #HEADER`. The title of the facet is the attribute `title` of the annotation and the subtitle is `description`. If the property of the data point is a property with unit of measure, the unit will be displayed in the footer. The attribute `measures` of the chart has to be a data point. \\n\\nThe `@UI.dataPoint` supports generally the attributes `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation`, but the support varies between the micro chart types. If the value of the data point is annotated with a unit of measure, the unit will be shown as the footer of the micro chart facet.\\n\\nIn the following examples, all used attributes are mandatory.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Area Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#AreaMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe area micro chart is a trend chart, which provides information for the actual and target value for a specified dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the dimension. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the measure attribute.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.chart: [\\n // Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n { \\n qualifier: 'areaChart',\\n title: 'Area Micro Chart (#AreaMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #AREA,\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForAreaChart'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nThe criticality calculation of the data point is mandatory, as each value is shown with its threshold (error, warning, acceptance and good) ranges.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForAreaChart',\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue',\\n criticalityCalculation: { \\n improvementDirection: #TARGET,\\n toleranceRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartTolLowerboundValue',\\n toleranceRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartTolUpperboundValue',\\n deviationRangeHighValueElement: 'AreachartDevUpperboundValue',\\n deviationRangeLowValueElement: 'AreachartDevLowerboundValue'\\n }\\n}\\nIntegerValueForAreaChart;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #AreaMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'areaChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/BTP/923180ddb98240829d935862025004d6/c7f12219d533404f8fad96aa68fa4ba6.html)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Bullet Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#BulletMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe bullet chart features a single measure and compares it to one or more other measures (e.g. value with target comparison). Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. The bullet chart does not support the criticality value of 5 (new item). The `measuresAttributes`, while it is mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\nChart\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart, #BulletMicroChartTable\\n@UI.chart: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'bulletChart',\\n title: 'Bullet Micro Chart (#BulletMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #BULLET,\\n measures: ['IntegerValue'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'IntegerValue',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nFacet\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #BulletMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER,\\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'bulletChart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. The attribute `minimumValue` is needed to render the chart properly. The value is the actual bar. The `forecastValue` is the bar in the background with a lower opacity and the `targetValue` is the dark line. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term: #BulletMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'IntegerValue', //IntegerValue: horizontal bar in relation to the goal line\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //visual goal line in the UI\\n forecastValue: 'ForecastValue', //horizontal bar behind the value bar with, slightly larger with higher transparency\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode',\\n minimumValue: 0 //Minimal value, needed for output rendering\\n}\\nIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Radial Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#RadialMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe radial micro chart displays a single percentage value. The `measureAttributes`, while mandatory, has no effect on the chart as the values come from datapoint.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\nChart\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n {\\n qualifier: 'radialChart',\\n title: 'Radial Micro Chart (#RadialMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #DONUT,\\n measures: ['RadialIntegerValue'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n {\\n measure: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nFacet\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type : #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'radialChart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. The percentage value is the fraction of the property value and the target value. The unit of measure label will not be rendered, as the chart displays percentage values. Both `criticality` and `criticalityCalculation` are supported, but if both are given `criticality` overrides `criticalityCalculation`. **Qualifier must be set to the name of the property.**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #RadialMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'RadialIntegerValue',\\n targetValueElement: 'TargetValue', //The relation between the value and the target value will be displayed as a percentage\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nRadialIntegerValue;\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Line Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#LineMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nThe line chart displays a series of data points as a line. The bottom values are the border values of the `dimension`. The upper left value is the smallest value of the first `measures` property and the upper right value is the largest value of the last `measures` property. The shown unit of measure is from the first entry.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n { \\n qualifier: 'lineChart',\\n title: 'Line Micro Chart (#LineMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #LINE,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForLineChart', 'TargetValue'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n },\\n { \\n measure: 'TargetValue',\\n role: #AXIS_2,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Points. It is recommended to only use one measure, and a maximum of three measures, if more is required. If the attribute `criticality` contains a path, then the value of the last data point's `criticality` determines the color of the line.\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI: {\\n dataPoint: {\\n qualifier: 'TargetValue',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n }\\n}\\nTargetValue;\\n\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.dataPoint: { \\n qualifier: 'IntegerValueForLineChart',\\n criticality: 'CriticalityCode'\\n}\\nIntegerValueForLineChart;\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #LineMicroChart\\n@UI.facet: [\\n {\\n purpose: #HEADER, \\n type: #CHART_REFERENCE,\\n targetQualifier: 'lineChart',\\n targetElement: '_Chart'\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nMore Information: [ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model: Visualizing Data with Charts](https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-abap-restful-application-programming-model/visualizing-data-with-charts)\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Column Micro Chart\\n\\n*Search term:* `#ColumnMicroChart`\\n\\n\\n\\nA column chart uses vertical bars to compare values of a dimension. The displayed values at the bottom of the chart are the boundary values of the `dimensions`. The values above the chart are the boundary values of the `measureAttributes`.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_ChartTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #ColumnMicroChart\\n@UI.chart: [\\n { \\n qualifier: 'columnChart',\\n title: 'Column Micro Chart (#ColumnMicroChart)',\\n description: 'This is a micro chart',\\n chartType: #COLUMN,\\n measures: ['IntegerValueForOtherCharts'],\\n dimensions: ['Dimensions'],\\n measureAttributes: [\\n { \\n measure: 'IntegerValueForOtherCharts',\\n role: #AXIS_1,\\n asDataPoint: true\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\nData Point. However you can define a factory action in the BDEF that copies an instance, and by using the keyword `isCopyAction: true`, the action button will be rendered in UI harmoniously with Edit/Delete.\\n\\nThe cardinality of a factory action is always [1], that means, only one instance can be copied at a time.\\n\\nWhen no label is provided, it is automatically set to 'Copy'. \\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n@UI.identification: [\\n // Search Term #OPCopyAction\\n {\\n type:#FOR_ACTION,\\n dataAction: 'copyInstance',\\n isCopyAction: true\\n }\\n]\\n```\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Behaviour Definition **/DMO/FSA_R_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\nfactory action copyInstance [1];\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Regular Actions\\n\\n*Search term:* `#OPHeaderAction`\\n\\nActions for the Object Page in general are annotated using `@UI.identification`. For example the delete operation can be dependent on a property of the entity, through the annotation `@UI.deleteHidden`. Fixed values true or false are also possible.\\n\\n> [!NOTE] \\n> Source: Metadata Extension **/DMO/FSA_C_RootTP**\\n\\n```\\n// Search Term #DynamicCRUD\\n@UI.updateHidden: #(UpdateHidden)\\n@UI.deleteHidden: #(DeleteHidden)\\n```\\n\\n:arrow_up_small: [Back to Content](#content)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Datafield WithAction in Object Page\\n\\n*Search term:* `#WithActionInOP`\\n\\n> [!WARNING] \\n> Only available with the latest [SAP BTP or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition release](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-cloud) and for SAP S/4HANA, on-premise edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, from [release 2023](https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/tree/ABAP-platform-2023) onwards.\\n\\n\\n\\nAn action that is tied to a data value, which would be rendered as a hyperlink. thinking.text:
Fixes needed: ZC_DM_PROJECT, ZC_DM_TASK, ZC_DM_TIMEENTRY DDLS require @Search adjustments. The DDLS projections for ZC_DM_PROJECT, ZC_DM_TASK, and ZC_DM_TIMEENTRY need @Search.defaultSearchElement set on at least one field, preferably Title, since searchable is currently only defined in DDLX. 

For ZME_DM_PROJECT, change criticality from integer 3 to string '3', or use an enum path instead.

Since @Search.defaultSearchElement requires @Search.searchable on the view, and searchable is defined in DDLS while DDLX references search annotations, either remove @Search.defaultSearchElement from DDLX only or add an annotate block for searchable. The simplest fix is to remove @Search. Remove @Search.defaultSearchElement from ZME_DM_PROJECT and place it only on DDLS elements. For ZC_DM_PROJECT, add @Search.defaultSearchElement to at least one field in the DDLS projection, preferably Title or ProjectId. Since @Search.annotations require 'searchable' for the view and searchable is defined in DDLS while DDLX references search annotations, remove @Search from DDLX entirely and add it to DDLS on fields. Also, fix criticality in ZME_DM_PROJECT by using a string literal like '3' or an enum path such as 'CriticalityCode'.
